item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic and SpaceX Are Shaking Up VC Fundraising
item.description: The potential for big gains allows firms to raise record amounts of money

item.title: US Corporate Profits Hit Levels That Alarmed Harry Truman’s Aides
item.description: US company profits are booming, but in the past such bonanzas have triggered angst over the economy.

item.title: A Veteran Apple Designer Reimagines the Golf Cart
item.description: Julian Hoenig’s Amble is dreaming up next-generation course cruisers — and aiming far beyond the fairway.

item.title: ECB Rate-Hike Forecasts Pared Back as Oil Regains Pre-War Level
item.description: A sharp drop in oil prices amid progress in Middle-East peace talks has prompted some economists to scale back expectations for European Central Bank interest-rate increases.

item.title: Micron Delivers the AI Reassurance Wall Street Was Craving
item.description: The US memory-chip maker released a quarterly sales forecast that crushed estimates, easing AI fears.

item.title: Japan Unveils Multi-Year Framework as Part of Budget Reform
item.description: Japan will create a new multi-year framework to finance policies aimed at boosting its growth potential as part of Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s overhaul of the country’s budgeting process.

item.title: Gilt Returns Climb to Three-Month High After Oil-Fueled Recovery
item.description: Returns on UK government bonds are the highest in three months as tumbling oil prices offset investor concerns about unpredictable politics.

item.title: AI IPO Wealth Seen Bolstering Luxury Industry Hurt by Iran War
item.description: The luxury sector is set to grow more slowly than previously predicted because of the Iran war, but wealth creation from the SpaceX listing and future US share sales by OpenAI and Anthropic could cushion the blow for struggling brands selling pricey handbags and watches, according to Bain & Co.

item.title: Russia Used Cellebrite Technology in Case Against Activist, Report Says
item.description: Russian authorities used technology manufactured by Israeli digital forensics company Cellebrite to aid the prosecution of a prominent pro-democracy activist, according to a new report.

item.title: NLB Chief Touts 40% Premium, Heritage in Bidding War for Addiko
item.description: After two years of trying to get a foothold in neighboring Croatia by buying Addiko Bank AG, Slovenia’s largest lender isn’t ready to give up.

item.title: Switzerland’s Capital Plan for UBS Wins Wholehearted IMF Backing
item.description: The Swiss government’s plan to make UBS Group AG hold significantly more capital was praised wholeheartedly by the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Microsoft, Amazon Cloud Units Face Tough EU Rules for Big Tech
item.description: Microsoft Corp.’s Azure and Amazon Web Services face tough European Union guardrails after regulators said they should be targeted by the bloc’s Digital Markets Act.

item.title: SK Hynix ADR Plans Leave Arb Traders Waiting on One Key Answer
item.description: SK Hynix Inc.’s planned $29 billion US listing has arbitrage investors dissecting securities filings and peppering brokers with questions about a single unresolved issue: whether the Korean chipmaker’s American depository receipts can be freely exchanged for Seoul-listed shares, a feature that could determine whether price gaps persist between the two markets.

item.title: US Tech Stocks Set to Rally as Micron Outlook Fuels AI Optimism
item.description: US stock futures jumped as Micron Technology Inc.’s solid forecast reinforced confidence about demand for artificial intelligence-related chips.

item.title: SNB Must Stay Nimble for Hike or Cut to Negative Rate, IMF Says
item.description: The Swiss National Bank must stay ready to either raise or cut interest rates if economic circumstances require it, the International Monetary Fund said.

item.title: Saudis Set to Restart Ras Tanura Oil Exports as Gulf Flows Rise
item.description: Top oil exporter Saudi Arabia appears to be about to restart loading crude at the Ras Tanura terminal inside the Persian Gulf, more than a week after a US-Iran peace deal was struck to free up the region’s petroleum flows.

item.title: Turkey’s Karahan Said to Signal No Rush to Ease Before Late July
item.description: Turkish central bank Governor Fatih Karahan ruled out resuming weekly repo funding before the next interest rate-setting meeting on July 23, effectively locking in higher borrowing costs at least until then, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chinese Crushers Seek Australian Canola on Trade Pact Hopes
item.description: Chinese oilseed crushers have made inquiries for fresh cargoes of Australian canola in anticipation of a trade pact that could end a years-long freeze on supplies.

item.title: GoreBox Game Owner ‘Heartbroken’ by Philippine School Shooting
item.description: The developer of an online game that was banned by the Philippines after a deadly school shooting said he was “heartbroken” by the incident, pledging to cooperate with authorities in Manila.

item.title: Norway Set for Longer Pause After One More Hike, Survey Finds
item.description: Norway’s central bank will wait longer than previously expected to pivot to easing after a likely final hike next quarter, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: UK Modifies Proposed Steel Import Curb After Industry Complaints
item.description: The UK modified proposed steel safeguards after businesses that use the alloy warned that curbs designed to prop up the country’s ailing steel industry would damage their own production.

item.title: Iraq Warns It Might Quit OPEC If It Doesn’t Get Quota Hike
item.description: Iraq said it might one day consider leaving OPEC unless the group accommodates it with a higher oil production quota, just a few months after the United Arab Emirates quit the group so that it could pump more.

item.title: China Wants ‘Malicious’ Bondholders Curbed in Poor-Nation Relief
item.description: China called for global measures to curb what it described as “malicious litigation” by bondholders in debt relief cases, saying such action is needed to protect a Group of 20 restructuring mechanism for poor countries struggling to repay their loans.

item.title: Amazon Pledges Additional $13 Billion in India AI Outlays
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. added $13 billion to its planned India investments, accelerating its buildout of artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure in the world’s most populous country.

item.title: CME Plans to Launch Wind Derivatives for US, Europe, Australia
item.description: The Chicago Mercantile Exchange plans to launch wind derivatives across three continents, according to a person familiar with the matter, as the role of renewable energy in power generation grows.

item.title: How the 1994 World Cup Transformed the Business of Football Forever
item.description: America’s enduring influence on FIFA.

item.title: Odd Lots: How the 1994 World Cup Transformed Football (Podcast)
item.description: The last time the World Cup came to the US was 1994. Before then, the World Cup was an enormously popular event with surprisingly limited commercial significance; the 1990 tournament in Italy, for instance, lost money for broadcasters. But that all changed in 1994, when American companies sought to make their mark in the form of advertisements and sponsorships: firms like McDonalds, Mastercard, and General Motors saw the potential to reach a global audience through one of the world’s most watche

item.title: Engines and Elevators Lift German Deals Past $120 Billion
item.description: Germany’s dealmakers are seeing some of their long-wished-for transactions come to fruition, driving the country to one of its busiest years for mergers and acquisitions in decades.

item.title: SK Hynix ADR Seen Driving 30% Upside as Micron Gap Narrows
item.description: SK Hynix Inc.’s push for a US listing is spurring talk of another prize for shareholders: a potentially higher market value that may narrow its gap with US rival Micron Technology Inc.

item.title: Swiss President Will Meet US’s Jamieson Greer Amid Tariff Spat
item.description: Swiss President Guy Parmelin will meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer later this month for talks as the two countries aim to finalize a broader trade deal amid renewed tariff pressure from Washington.
